prd.problem: In-person deals settle in cash or bank transfer, so the buyer must send money before receiving the item — a moment of fraud exposure. The pricier the item, the more 'pay first' anxiety kills the deal
prd.solution: The app holds the payment in escrow and settles it to the seller once the buyer confirms receipt. It adds a safeguard where goods and money move together to in-person dealing
inScope: Settlement to the seller after the buyer confirms receipt
Safe-payment guidance entered from chat and deal status
nonGoals: Automated arbitration of refunds and disputes (deferred to manual support)
prd.goal: Raise the completion rate of high-value deals (over $80) by 20% versus before safe payment

scenarios.text: Jiyoung buys a $200 bike with safe payment, holds the amount in escrow, inspects it in person, then taps confirm receipt so it settles to the seller

requirements.title: Safe payment
requirements.desc: Hold the payment in escrow and settle after receipt confirmation

acceptance.text: Nothing settles to the seller before receipt is confirmed

features.title: Escrow payment
features.desc: Deal safely in three steps: hold, confirm receipt, settle

specs.title: Hold payment
specs.desc: When the buyer pays, the app holds the amount

acceptance.text: On hold, the seller is notified of the escrow

specs.title: Confirm receipt
specs.desc: The buyer confirms receipt of the item

specs.title: Settle
specs.desc: On receipt confirmation, settle the held amount to the seller

features.title: Safe-payment guidance
features.desc: Guide the use of safe payment from the deal-status screen
